sections: HCM-1.1 additive kind/schema registry boundary proof gate

repo_truth: Review the exact manifest over baseline 87bc3ab3e175aa1d6c058ac0ed57495989fe5451. Fresh Final Registry Reviews 1 through 6 returned five, three, four, two, two, and one Required findings; the parent accepted and remediated every Required finding within HCM-1.1. The two repeated non-blocking Optional observations remain unchanged. The new subject includes all six immutable prior review dispatches and all seven proof walls. Registry errors and structural locations are fail-safe bounded. Normalized UTF-8 schema paths and prewalked RFC 6901 fragments map injectively to private in-memory URIs, validator-only refs target exact prewalked resources, and reported paths/pointers decode exactly. Authored bytes and fingerprints remain unchanged. The selected repository root remains the caller-provided trust anchor while every relative component is descriptor-relative no-follow. Current canonical product paths still use the fixed four-variant CanonicalArtifactKind and do not call the new registry. Derive the verdict only from live authority, replayed bytes, and read-only verification.
allowed_scope: Remain read-only and replay every sorted subject-manifest entry plus aggregate before review.

Review every engine implementation, stable-role asset, test/fixture, HCM-1.1 packet/status/proof byte, all six prior immutable review dispatches, the single crosswalk row, and bounded gate-evidence change in the manifest.
Read unchanged canonical authority, entry handoff/ledger, live Cargo metadata, platform APIs, and official dependency documentation only as needed to validate the subject.
Run read-only focused, workspace, cross-target compile, package, dependency-feature, archive, handoff, Git, source, security, and semantic checks needed to evaluate the subject.
Return findings first through the built-in channel with exact file and line evidence.
non_goals: No edits, staging, commits, new dispatches, global handoffs, ledger writes, or user-task hops.

No HCM-1.2 or later-slice work and no canonical artifact, layout, setup, doctor, flow, compiler, CLI, SDK, Tauri, Substrate, profile, instance, intake, renderer, lifecycle, Projection, publication, or downstream adoption.
Do not infer missing first-party schemas, semantic-capability contracts, intake/renderer/lifecycle/Projection definitions, shipped profile assets, or product authority.
Do not accept unit proof as product-path adoption or close PG-KIND-01/PG-KIND-02.
Do not require stylistic alternatives without a violated authority, invariant, security boundary, proof gate, or concrete defect.
tasks: Invoke the required skills, remain read-only, replay the exact sorted manifest and aggregate, and return BLOCKED if any subject byte differs.

Review findings first in Critical, Required, Optional, Nit order. Each actionable finding must include severity, exact file/line, violated contract or invariant, why the boundary/proof is wrong, smallest valid remediation, and missing verification.
Verify all Review 1 through Review 6 Required findings are fully remediated and the latest proof accurately supersedes earlier overclaims without erasing immutable lineage.

Stress typed error boundaries with large duplicate YAML/JSON keys, unknown fields, unknown variants, malformed identities, invalid meta-shapes/regexes, paths, refs, and validation locations; prove no absolute machine path, upstream display text, authored sentinel, or unbounded content reaches detail, location, or Display.

Reproduce the Review 4 long noncanonical source-path and 100,000-byte exact-schema-ref probes; verify location and Display are bounded and contain no sentinel, and audit every dynamic exact/document location for a stable field label or the central fail-safe bound.

Reproduce the Review 5 valid 583+ byte document-path structural-error probe and require the stable schema_root fallback; verify no structural instance/schema location exceeds 512 bytes or echoes the sentinel.

Reproduce the Review 5 live space/UTF-8 closure and exact URI matrix; prove private URI encoding is injective and reversible, validator-only translated refs target the exact prewalked resources, authored bytes/fingerprints remain unchanged, and reported repo paths decode without drift.

Reproduce the Review 6 cross-document RFC 6901 fragment cases with space and UTF-8 tokens; prove injective private fragment encoding, exact decoded structural pointers, valid/invalid behavior, closure agreement, unchanged authored fingerprints, and continued authored-percent refusal.

Verify the caller-selected Unix repository root is an explicit trust anchor, including through a directory symlink, while every repo-relative component remains descriptor-relative no-follow; verify NONBLOCK on the final component, retained-handle regularity before reads, prompt FIFO refusal, and typed intermediate symlink versus ordinary non-directory classification.

Replay the canonical product real-root versus symlink-selected-root equality regression and the paired intermediate relative artifact-component symlink refusal; verify the trust-anchor repair does not weaken repo-relative no-follow enforcement.

Verify the shared reader split preserves legacy canonical-product behavior on non-Unix while strict registry access fails closed there; replay the x86_64-pc-windows-gnu engine-tests compile and do not claim native Windows execution.

Literally inspect and replay both custom entries and both custom kinds: forward/reverse registry fingerprints, exact lookup sets, exact closure member sets, each entry closure/definition fingerprint, each kind definition fingerprint, and valid/invalid behavior for both kinds.

Literally verify every source/document/reference bound, fingerprint drift, media/compatibility, root containment, schema meta/keyword/identifier policy, duplicate/conflict order, resolver exclusion, and later-owned dependency refusal required by SPEC.

Audit the rustix 1.1.4 fs dependency repair, target configuration, lockfile edge, descriptor error classification, retained-handle race tests, source budgets, and platform branches for containment, hangs, panics, portability claims, and unintended capability.

Verify the implementation matches the exact additive HCM-1.1 public owner boundary, source/asset topology, dependency pins/features, error contract, TDD evidence, proof wall, classification ceiling, and non-goals.

Audit jsonschema configuration and the resolved dependency graph for in-memory-only resources, disabled ambient resolver behavior, linear regex, and absence of HTTP/TLS/async/file/custom execution paths.

Verify the public API has exact-ref lookups and deterministic structural validation without an untyped dependency dispatcher; distinguish the two acknowledged Optional ergonomics/performance observations from Critical/Required contract defects.

Replay or inspect every positive, negative, security, engine/workspace, cross-target, feature-tree, package-content, archive, handoff, diff/scope, and GitNexus assertion in the latest proof; identify any asserted case not actually exercised or any required proof absent from the manifest.
Verify the crosswalk promotes only the additive owner-library seam to BoundaryLanded, PG-KIND-01 and PG-KIND-02 remain open, and no sibling gate or product-path claim is widened.
Return CLEAN only if no valid Critical or Required finding remains; otherwise return CHANGES_REQUIRED or BLOCKED. Make no edits.
stop_conditions: Return BLOCKED if the manifest, aggregate, entry authority, or required read-only verification cannot be reproduced.
Return CHANGES_REQUIRED rather than resolving a missing product, semantic-capability, lifecycle, intake, renderer, Projection, publication, or downstream decision by inference.
Return to the parent if remediation would require HCM-1.2+, a current product path, a sibling crate, or authority outside the exact HCM-1.1 packet.
